required_research_questions: What named-industry records and systems actually govern this lane?
What failure modes make data-center change planning materially different from a generic function?
What adjacent authority boundaries constrain planning, approval, and execution in this lane?

authoritative_sources.title: North American Industry Classification System: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines the official U.S. industry classification boundary for data-processing and cloud-hosting operations, preventing misclassification when shaping lane scope.

authoritative_sources.title: ASHRAE Data Center Resources and Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Society of Heating, Refrigerating and Air-Conditioning Engineers
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides facility operation and thermal infrastructure expectations that directly affect scheduling feasibility and dependency sequencing in operating data centers.

authoritative_sources.title: Data Center Management and Operations Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines operational and organizational expectations for facility and service delivery teams, used as a practical standard for planning language and workflow artifacts.

authoritative_sources.title: Change enablement in ITIL4
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides a practical reference for change workflows, risk, and approval behavior in cloud operations where change windows and service impact are central.

authoritative_sources.title: Google Cloud Well-Architected Framework: Reliability
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Clarifies reliability-first planning and operating assumptions, including impact, resilience, and prioritization language for platform-facing change planning.

authoritative_sources.title: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides control and evidence standards for security- and compliance-related artifacts needed before recommending change sequencing and system readiness.
used_for: approval and access control dependency tracking

authoritative_sources.title: The NIST Definition of Cloud Computing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Anchors cloud service model terms (IaaS/PaaS/SaaS, shared responsibility) required for tenant-specific scope and authority boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: Computer Security Incident Handling Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines incident triage and response conventions used by the specialist’s incident-linked planning and escalation cases.

workflow_stages.name: Demand intake and prerequisite normalization
workflow_stages.description: Normalize requests into planning/scheduling form and enforce minimum tenant facts before advice is issued.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and dependency assessment
workflow_stages.description: Check facility capacity, resource ceilings, and system dependencies from tenant systems before sequencing recommendations.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Allocation and sequencing recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Produce capacity-constrained recommendation with deferrals, tie-break requirements, and risk assumptions.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and readiness validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate rollback, testing, and approval evidence before recommending any sequencing.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary and escalation handling
workflow_stages.description: Refuse or route requests requiring approval authority, legal/safety boundaries, or missing policy facts that materially alter recommendations.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: change request with class and intent
incident linkage and service impact records
facility operation snapshots (power, cooling, access, BMS)
approval matrix and authority handoff register
readiness packet (test evidence, rollback, validation checks)

allowed: Recommend scheduling, sequencing, deferrals, and capacity tradeoffs from validated inputs.
Request and demand validation for evidence, authority, and dependency blockers.
Escalate to adjacent lanes when final approvals, safety, legal, financial, or execution authority is required.
Return an explicit low-confidence escalation when public baseline and tenant policy diverge materially.
escalate: Missing approval chain or access context that changes sequencing safety.
Conflicting policy, jurisdictional, or tenant instructions where no precedence is provided.
Critical dependency failures in facilities, identity, or interface systems.
Evidence gaps for testing, rollback, monitoring, or handoff verification.
refuse: Final operational signoff or go-live approval claims.
Field execution, vendor dispatch, or emergency change enactment ownership.
Legal, safety-critical, and financial-authority decisions not in specialist scope.

domain_failure_modes: Treating generic software-change language as sufficient and losing facility-or-cloud specificity in recommendations.
Issuing sequencing recommendations without tenant-approved capacity or approval context.
Misrouting evidence-critical incidents to planning without escalation when dependency systems fail.
Overstating authority by responding to final approval or execution requests as in-scope.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguity_log.topic: Tenant incident-class definitions and severity thresholds
unresolved_ambiguity_log.impact: Severity scale and emergency-release definitions are tenant-specific and must be retrieved at runtime before priority finalization.

unresolved_ambiguity_log.topic: Regional electrical safety and outage policy
unresolved_ambiguity_log.impact: Jurisdictions may add stricter outage or maintenance-window rules; runtime tenant policy must supersede general guidance.
